topPlaces.name: Hubli–Dharwad
topPlaces.description: Hubli (or Hubballi) and Dharwad are twin cities in the Indian state of Karnataka. Hubli-Dharwad form the second-largest municipality and urban agglomeration of Karnataka in terms of population, after the capital Bangalore.

topPlaces.name: Chandramauleshwara Temple, Unkal
topPlaces.description: The Chandramauleshwara Temple, sometimes referred to as the Candramauḷĩśvara or Chandramouleshwara temple at Unkal, is an 11th-century Shiva temple with Chalukyan architecture in Unkal (Hubballi), Karnataka, India. It is built on a square-cross plan with one entrance for each cardinal direction, corresponding to the sandhara plan found in Sanskrit texts on architecture.

topPlaces.name: Kendriya Vidyalaya, Hubli
topPlaces.description: Kendriya Vidyalaya No.1 Rajnagar, Hubli is a school in Hubli, Karnataka, India, established in 1965 to provide primary and higher Secondary (10+2) education to the children of Central Government employees and also others of Hubli city. The school follows the syllabus of the Central Board of Secondary Education, New Delhi.

topPlaces.name: Tarihal
topPlaces.description: Tarihal is a village in Dharwad district of Karnataka, India.

topPlaces.name: Lakamanahalli
topPlaces.description: Lakamanahalli is a suburb in Dharwad city, Dharwad district of Karnataka, India. It is an industrial area for small scale industries which are involved in clothing, silk, textile and rubber moulding.

topPlaces.name: Hubli taluk
topPlaces.description: Hubli Taluka is a taluka located in Dharwad District of the Indian state of Karnataka.

topPlaces.name: Gopankop
topPlaces.description: Gopankop is a village in Dharwad district of Karnataka, India. According to Census 2011 information the location code or village code of Gopankop village is 602377.

topPlaces.name: Bengeri
topPlaces.description: Bengeri is a village in the Hubballi City of Karnataka, India. It is well known for its national famous "Khadi Gramodyog" i.e.

items.note: Hubli (or Hubballi) and Dharwad are twin cities in the Indian state of Karnataka. Hubli-Dharwad form the second-largest municipality and urban agglomeration of Karnataka in terms of population, after the capital Bangalore.

The Chandramauleshwara Temple, sometimes referred to as the Candramauḷĩśvara or Chandramouleshwara temple at Unkal, is an 11th-century Shiva temple with Chalukyan architecture in Unkal (Hubballi), Karnataka, India. It is built on a square-cross plan with one entrance for each cardinal direction, corresponding to the sandhara plan found in Sanskrit texts on architecture.

Kendriya Vidyalaya No.1 Rajnagar, Hubli is a school in Hubli, Karnataka, India, established in 1965 to provide primary and higher Secondary (10+2) education to the children of Central Government employees and also others of Hubli city. The school follows the syllabus of the Central Board of Secondary Education, New Delhi.
Tarihal is a village in Dharwad district of Karnataka, India.
Lakamanahalli is a suburb in Dharwad city, Dharwad district of Karnataka, India. It is an industrial area for small scale industries which are involved in clothing, silk, textile and rubber moulding.
Hubli Taluka is a taluka located in Dharwad District of the Indian state of Karnataka.
Gopankop is a village in Dharwad district of Karnataka, India. According to Census 2011 information the location code or village code of Gopankop village is 602377.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Banashankari Temple, Amargol?
faq.a: The best time to visit Banashankari Temple, Amargol is Nov – Feb. Summers are 26–36°C and winters are 22–32°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Banashankari Temple, Amargol?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Hubli Airport (4 km) and the nearest railway station is Hubballi Junction (8 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Banashankari Temple, Amargol?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Banashankari Temple, Amargol at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Banashankari Temple, Amargol?
faq.a: Stays in Banashankari Temple, Amargol start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Banashankari Temple, Amargol?
faq.a: The top attractions are Hubli–Dharwad, Chandramauleshwara Temple, Unkal, Kendriya Vidyalaya, Hubli, Tarihal, Lakamanahalli.
